ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Reserve
Off Calendar Jun-06-2023 Continued To Aug-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-01-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Chris Cole, via Zoom, for Leslie Actkinson, Conservator of the Estate and Trustee.

The court and counsel discuss the extraordinary fees requested in the trust matter. Counsel reports on those fees and advises that similar requests have been granted in the previous accountings and that he mistakenly refers to them as extraordinary fees in the instant petition. Counsel offers clarification regarding the additional fees requested, questioned by the Examiner. The court and counsel discuss the fee requests in the conservatorship matter. Counsel offers advocacy for the fee requests and advises that the request for $190 is withdrawn; the request for $456.25 should be $356.25; and offers clarification regarding the fee request for ex parte applications which should be separated.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Glisson. Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-12-296723 Conservatorship of Jennie Actkinson and
PTR-12-295908 The Jennie Actkinson Trust

Appearances: Christopher Cole appeared by zoom for Leslee Actkinson (Trustee and Conservator of the estate).

Mr. Cole informed the Court that Ms. Gerard is not feeling well. The Court noted that the issues are the necessity of getting Court approval prior to transferring funds from the conservatorship to the trust, and the increase in payments for care, and room and board, and stated that it finds that the amounts are reasonable, but there is not a comfort level in just doing it and getting back approval, and that they need to get prior Court approval. Mr. Cole stated that they will do so going forward. The Fourth Report and Account of Conservator, etc. and the Fourth Report and Account of Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-12-295723 Conservatorship of Jennie Actkinson and PTR-12-295908 The Jennie Actkinson Trust.

Appearances: Christopher Cole for Leslee Actkinson (Conservator of the Estate/Trustee).

Mr. Cole informed the Court that the accounting in the Trust matter was filed yesterday with a hearing date of April 9, 2018, that he does not have the information for the Conservatorship, that he expects to have the accounting within a week and that he will then file the petition with the same hearing date as the Trust. Status on filing of the third account in the Trust matter is off calendar. Status on filing of the third account in the conservatorship matter is continued to March 12, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting petition is to be given a hearing date of April 9th.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Cole for (petitioner) Leslee Actkinson.

The Court stated that this is a second report and request for fees, that in regards to the attorney's fees there are a number of entries and substantial time for the attorney attending open houses, assembling documents and things that the trustee should be doing. Mr. Cole stated that the conservator is out of state, that he could not attend the open houses and does not have the financial/business acumen to do this. The Court stated yet he requests full payment and is charging the trust for things that the trustee should have handled. Mr. Cole stated that there were unusual circumstances, that he had to act quickly to save money, that the trustee was appointed due to family issues and that the trust would have had to pay out hundreds of thousands of dollars in taxes. The Court stated that is questions if it is fair and reasonable to charge the trust full rate for the trustee and necessary work at attorney's rates when it was a trustee's job and not requiring a professional. Mr. Cole stated that he disagrees, that he went to view the houses and condo's to advise the trustee if they were suitable for the trust as an investment, that in regards to the trustee's time they were on the phone and emailing back and forth constantly, that reports were required and that it required attorney knowledge not real estate knowledge. The Court stated that the trustee is seeking travel reimbursement of $360.00 without support. Mr. Cole stated that he has had conversations with the trustee regarding keeping receipts, that he is not used to doing such and that he has been told that the Court may not approve it. The accounting petition was taken under submission to review the fees.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-16-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Cole for (trustee & petitioner) Leslee Actkinson.

The Court asked for an update on the 1031 exchange. Mr. Cole reported that the year has run out, that the option was exercised, that they are pressing to sign papers, that the house will be cleared out and papers signed the week of 6/22 and addressed the statement in the petition of "transaction if and only a transfer" that it is not accurate and that it is intended as a transfer but could just turn into a sale. Mr. Cole further reported that they have 45 days from the close of escrow to designate the property being purchased through the 1031 exchange, that the broker has been providing them with a list of available properties weekly, that they will most likely choose a residential condo and that there is no immediate need for funds needed by the conservatee. The Court asked what the benefit would be of the transfer to the current beneficiary. Mr. Cole stated that there may be no benefit but if they do not have to pay the tax now they may not need to pay it in the future. The Court expressed concern regarding the expense of this process and that there are substantial attorney plus the expert tax attorney expenses to the trust. Mr. Cole stated that they are saving in brokers fees, that the expert tax attorney did not charge for reviewing the contracts, that the contract is at the expense of the purchaser rather than the trust and that he himself is familiar with 1031 exchanges and that most of his hours billed reflected time spent discussing matters with the conservatee and dealing with the construction next door to the property. Mr. Cole reported on the fees for the trustee, that the hourly fee is too low for this responsibility, that the guideline fee of 1% is appropriate, that the trustee walked into a horrible situation, has done his best to clear the house and keep everyone happy, has had to juggle offers and that his only goal is his mother's welfare and that the trustee will not object to whatever the court decides. The Court stated that it is likely to approve the 1% fee less the $2000.00, that future accountings should be less complicated, that hereafter the hourly fee should be the appropriate rate of compensation and asked about the time entries to obtain records that are being kept by the conservator. Mr. Cole stated that it makes sense to have the trust offices in California to be where the conservator is, that he has spoken with Mr. Actkinson about having the two trust accounts online (and said one already is), that they previously had issues obtaining records until they requested them but are now getting them on a monthly basis which includes a letter regarding expenses. The Court addressed the $21,000.00 in fees from litigation. Mr. Cole stated that he has a copy of the invoice (and provided a copy to the clerk) and will file a supplemental declaration. The accounting petition was taken under submission awaiting the filing of the supplemental declaration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
